author: 𝕁𝕒𝕪
content:
Bro, you lost $30k over 5 years. That’s $6k a year. $500’a month. $125 a week. $75 a day.  

Look at it as your 5 year tuition to learn practice and perfect.  

Are you trading to earn a living. Is it a hobby or are you trading solely to get your losses back ? 

Figure out why you’re trading. You can’t make up 5 years in 1 day or 1 week.  Has it been done in a week or a month SURE. But if we get stuck in a mindset we know is wrong yet are only aware of it after we go against it then the losses are gone and there was no tuition. It was essentially gambling.  

I been there, believe me. I’ve blown $35k in less than 2 months.  I feel your pain. A lot of us do.  

If you make $75 a day and shut her down you will get your losses back in time.  
You’re going to have good days and bad obviously but if your RM is there and you’re actually journaling and going over your journal weekly at least it’s going to make it.  

Anyone can make a trade, we have to find trades.  
All Love Bro. Hope you’re able to get a handle on it all and breathe.

author: snowspecs
content:
Mah charts for today:

NQ having a big push down here before open, breaking the support we've been riding since early this week. Still overall consolidation until we either break 19.6 or 20.1. We've already taken out the previous day low, but we've got a ton of liq built up for an overall push lower. real good long opp would be a bounce off 19.6 for a retrace off this premarket move. 

ES: Breaking all local trend support as well, already taking out PDLs. 5651 and 5616 would be the levels im watching for the biggest reversal. Probably looking at short bias on open to ride it down to these levels

GC: Wicking off a 15m OB currently, creating a local downtrend after reaching aths earlier this week. Really strong 4H support level at 3035, but need to break the TL to continue higher. A push down to 3035 and a move up to test it at that OB would be my ideal look early am.

author: unnas
content: I took both trades today on TSLA one contract each and ended up with a loss of $60. I was really confused at first, but I went back and analyzed the execution. From what I think happened is that the first trade had a loss of about $180 per contract due to the second down closed candle pushing so far below the stop loss. Since we have to wait for the candle to close, we get hammered on these losses. Waiting for the candle to close has its pros and cons, but overall Tony recommends to wait. And since I only trade one contract,  I always get out at TP1, which is usually the HOD.  I don't think you or I did anything wrong, it was more of a lopsided trade.

author: MovaMove
content: I could’ve been wrong, but in the five minute, I noticed the Q’s was trying to break HOD and forming a flat top simultaneously. I was waiting for it to break above the flat top with strong displacement. Once that happened, I switched to the one minute for my entry. I waited for it to come back and retest the flat top, which also aligned with high of day. It came back and retested the flat top with strong price action. I entered at the 1:16 candle. TP was originally supposed to be the 430 sych level, which is also a previous day pivot level. SL right below the flat top. Fortunately I was not quick enough to sell at that level😂. One of my best trades so far.  Not in terms of the profit, but the way I planned it, and patiently waited to execute.

author: Kam
content:
hey <@1323448182851637345>, I'm assuming your trade was a BnR off the 5m opening range. a few points to consider: 

1) on higher timeframes we opened up right in the middle of yesterday's range on the ES, so in order to go long or short at an intraday level like the 5m OR, you ideally need stronger evidence of momentum in either direction. Try zooming out to the 15m or 1hr timeframe to see the bigger picture 

2) the amount of displacement is key for the BnR system. In this case there wasn't a strong push or super strong candle close above the level you're using for the retest. Note the upper wicks (a tell for sellers) on the push above the 5m range on the 1m chart

3) if your entry was on the 9:57 candle, it's likely you didn't wait until the candle closed for confirmation that your level was holding. Remember you want to see strong price action at your level in order to enter. 

Will post a couple of links to videos Jack has made recently that may help with building higher timeframe narratives for your trade ideas as well as good entry confirmation signals
